_comment: Annotated CoreVital trace excerpts from two real runs (2026-02-28). Each trace is trimmed to ONE generation step with _comment fields explaining every section. Source traces: runs/trace_7aaa4ac3.json (CausalLM) and runs/trace_0c9ed9cc.json (Seq2Seq).

causal_lm_example._comment: CausalLM trace from meta-llama/Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct. Decoder-only architecture: no encoder_layers, no cross_attention. Timeline trimmed to 1 of 128 generation steps; layer arrays trimmed to 3 of 28 layers.
causal_lm_example._comment_schema_version: Semantic version of the CoreVital report schema. Current version is 0.4.0 (Phase-2).
causal_lm_example._comment_trace_id: UUID v4 unique to this run, generated at report creation time.
causal_lm_example._comment_created_at_utc: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the report was finalized.

model._comment: Metadata about the model under test. Captured automatically from the HuggingFace model config.
model._comment_hf_id: HuggingFace model identifier used to load the model.
model._comment_revision: Git commit SHA of the model weights on HuggingFace Hub.
model._comment_architecture: Model class name. CausalLM = decoder-only autoregressive. Other types: T5ForConditionalGeneration (Seq2Seq).
model._comment_num_layers: Number of transformer decoder layers. Each layer has self-attention + FFN. Determines the size of layers[] in each timeline step.
model._comment_hidden_size: Dimensionality of hidden state vectors. Affects magnitude of L2 norms and max_abs values.
model._comment_num_attention_heads: Number of self-attention heads per layer. Collapse/focus counts are out of this total.
model._comment_dtype: Tensor data type. 'quantized_unknown' means 4-bit quantization was used but the exact internal dtype could not be detected.
model._comment_quantization: Quantization reduces memory usage but can affect numerical precision, potentially increasing anomalies.

run_config._comment: Snapshot of the configuration used for this run. Fully reproducible with same seed + config.
run_config._comment_max_new_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate. The model may stop earlier if it produces an EOS token.

generation._comment: Sampling parameters. temperature < 1.0 sharpens the distribution; top_k and top_p are nucleus-sampling cutoffs.

hidden._comment: Which hidden-state summary statistics to compute per layer per step.

sketch._comment: Random-projection sketch for optional dimensionality-reduced hidden state fingerprints. Not used in current analysis but reserved for future clustering.

attention._comment: Which attention summary statistics to compute per layer per step.

logits._comment: Which logits metrics to compute per step. topk=5 means the top 5 candidate tokens are stored.

sink._comment: Where the trace JSON is written. Alternatives: 'sqlite', 'wandb', etc.

prompt._comment: The input prompt fed to the model. token_ids are the tokenizer output; num_tokens = len(token_ids).
prompt.text: List five speculative but plausible advances in quantum error correction by 2030. For each, one sentence only.
prompt._comment_token_ids: Actual array has 23 integer token IDs. First 8 shown above; see full trace for exact values.

generated._comment: The model's output. token_ids are decoder outputs (including any special tokens); num_tokens = len(token_ids).
generated.output_text: 1. **Fault-tolerant quantum processors**: A new class of quantum processors capable of correcting errors in the quantum computation process, using a combination of quantum error correction codes and
generated._comment_token_ids: Actual array has 128 integer token IDs. First 8 shown above.

timeline._comment: ONE generation step shown (of 128 total). step_index = prompt_tokens + generation_position. For this CausalLM, prompt has 23 tokens, so the first generated token is step_index 23.

token._comment: The token produced at this step. is_prompt_token is always false in the generation timeline.

logits_summary._comment: Metrics computed from the full logit distribution at this step, BEFORE sampling.
logits_summary._comment_entropy: Shannon entropy in bits. Low (0.84) = very confident. Range: 0 to ~log2(vocab_size). < 2 confident, 2-4 normal, > 4 uncertain.
logits_summary._comment_topk: Deprecated alias for topk_probs. Both contain the top-K most probable tokens with their probabilities.
logits_summary._comment_topk_probs: Top-K candidate tokens sorted by probability. prob values sum to topk_mass. Here the top token dominates at 81.6%.
logits_summary._comment_top_k_margin: Probability gap between rank-1 and rank-2 token. High (0.68) = clear winner. Range: [0, 1]. Close to 0 = model nearly tied between two choices.
logits_summary._comment_voter_agreement: Deprecated alias for topk_mass. Kept for backward compatibility.
logits_summary._comment_topk_mass: Sum of top-K probabilities. 1.0 = all probability mass is in the top-5 tokens. Range: [0, 1]. Low values indicate probability spread across many tokens.
logits_summary._comment_perplexity: 2^entropy. Effective number of equiprobable tokens. 1.79 means the model is choosing among ~2 plausible options. Range: [1, vocab_size].
logits_summary._comment_surprisal: -log2(prob of chosen token). Low (0.29) = the chosen token was highly expected. Range: [0, ∞). Spikes indicate unexpected token choices.

layers._comment: Layer 0 of 28 decoder layers. First layer processes raw token embeddings.

hidden_summary._comment: Summary statistics of the hidden-state tensor after this layer's forward pass.
hidden_summary._comment_mean: Mean of all hidden-state values. Near zero is typical for well-behaved layers due to LayerNorm.
hidden_summary._comment_std: Standard deviation. Low for early layers; grows deeper in the network.
hidden_summary._comment_l2_norm_mean: Mean L2 norm of hidden vectors across sequence positions. Grows with depth. Used for explosion detection.
hidden_summary._comment_max_abs: Largest absolute value in the hidden tensor. Extreme values may indicate numerical instability.
hidden_summary._comment_sketch: Random-projection sketch vector (empty when dim=32 but not populated for this run).
hidden_summary._comment_clipped: True if values were clamped to ±1e6 for numerical stability before computing stats.

attention_summary._comment: Self-attention statistics aggregated across all 24 heads at this layer for the current query position.
attention_summary._comment_entropy_mean: Mean Shannon entropy (nats) of attention distributions across heads. Higher = more diffuse attention.
attention_summary._comment_entropy_mean_normalized: entropy_mean / log(seq_length), normalized to [0, 1]. 1.0 = uniform attention; 0.0 = perfectly peaked. Used for collapse detection (< 0.03 = collapsed).
attention_summary._comment_entropy_min: Lowest entropy across all heads. Very low = at least one head is almost entirely focused on one key.
attention_summary._comment_concentration_max: Highest max-attention-weight across all heads. Near 1.0 = one head has almost all weight on a single token.
attention_summary._comment_entropy_max: Highest entropy across all heads. Shows the most diffuse head.
attention_summary._comment_concentration_min: Lowest max-attention-weight. Low value = that head's attention is very spread out.
attention_summary._comment_collapsed_head_count: Heads with normalized entropy < 0.03. Out of 24 total heads. Some collapse is normal, especially in early/late layers.
attention_summary._comment_focused_head_count: Heads with concentration > 0.9. These are 'specialist' heads attending strongly to specific tokens.

anomalies._comment: NaN/Inf detection per layer. If true, something is critically wrong with this layer's computation.

layers._comment: Layer 14 of 28 (middle layer). Layers 1-13 omitted for brevity — same structure as layer 0.

hidden_summary._comment: Note L2 norm grew from 2.6 (layer 0) to 44.9 (layer 14). This growth is normal — hidden states accumulate information with depth.

attention_summary._comment: No collapsed heads in this middle layer, but 5 focused heads — healthy mid-network attention pattern.

layers._comment: Layer 27 of 28 (final decoder layer). Layers 15-26 omitted for brevity.

hidden_summary._comment: L2 norm peaked at 83.5. The final layer feeds into the language model head for next-token prediction.

timeline._comment_layers: 28 decoder layers total; only layers 0, 14, 27 shown. Every layer has the same structure: hidden_summary, attention_summary, anomalies, extensions. CausalLM layers have NO cross_attention field (that is Seq2Seq only).

causal_lm_example._comment_timeline: Full trace has 128 steps (one per generated token). step_index runs from 23 (first generated token) to 150. Only step 23 shown.

summary._comment_total_steps: prompt_tokens + generated_tokens = 23 + 128 = 151. This counts all positions processed.
summary._comment_elapsed_ms: Wall-clock time for the instrumented generation (excluding model load).

causal_lm_example._comment_warnings: List of Warning objects {code, message}. Empty = no issues detected during capture.
causal_lm_example._comment_encoder_layers: Always null for CausalLM (decoder-only) models. Only populated for Seq2Seq models like T5.

prompt_analysis._comment: Telemetry from an extra forward pass over the prompt (Phase-1b). Captures how the model 'reads' the prompt before generation begins.
layers: ... (28 PromptAttentionLayer objects, one per decoder layer)
prompt_analysis._comment_layers: Each layer contains: 'heads' (sparse attention in SoA format: query_indices, key_indices, weights for significant connections) and 'basin_scores' (per-head ratio of middle-third vs boundary attention). Example basin_scores from layer 0: [0.1521, 0.4307, 0.0214, ...]. Scores < 0.3 suggest 'lost in the middle' for that head.
prompt_analysis._comment_layer_transformations: Cosine distance between input and output hidden states for each layer transition. High values = layer transforms its input significantly. 27 values for 28 layers (layer-to-layer transitions).
prompt_analysis._comment_prompt_surprisals: -log2(prob) for each prompt token given its prefix. High values at the start are normal (model has less context). One value per prompt token excluding the first (22 = 23 - 1).

health_flags._comment: Aggregated boolean health indicators from post-processing (Phase-1c). Quick pass/fail checks.
health_flags._comment_nan_detected: True if ANY layer at ANY step had NaN values. Catastrophic — risk goes to 1.0.
health_flags._comment_inf_detected: True if ANY layer at ANY step had Inf values. Catastrophic — risk goes to 1.0.
health_flags._comment_attention_collapse_detected: True if any layer has collapsed_head_count > 0 in any step. Mild concern (risk floor 0.15).
health_flags._comment_high_entropy_steps: Count of generation steps where entropy exceeded the model-specific threshold (default 4.0 bits). 2 out of 128 steps = low concern.
health_flags._comment_repetition_loop_detected: True if last-layer hidden states showed cosine similarity > 0.9995 for 3+ consecutive steps. Strong risk signal (floor 0.9).
health_flags._comment_mid_layer_anomaly_detected: True if NaN/Inf or L2 norm explosion detected in the middle third of layers. Risk floor 0.7.

extensions._comment: Extensible key-value store for Phase-2+ analytics computed from the raw timeline data.

compound_signals.name: confident_repetition_risk
compound_signals.description: Model is extremely confident with near-unanimous token selection — may be locked into a repetitive pattern.
compound_signals._comment: Compound signals detect multi-metric patterns. Each has: name (machine ID), description (human-readable), severity (0-1, added to risk score), evidence (supporting data).

extensions._comment_compound_signals: Possible signals: context_loss (0.6), confident_confusion (0.5), degenerating_generation (0.5), attention_bottleneck (0.4), confident_repetition_risk (0.3).

risk._comment: Composite risk assessment combining health flags, continuous metrics, and compound signals into a single 0-1 score.
risk._comment_risk_score: 0-1. < 0.3 low risk, 0.3-0.7 moderate, > 0.7 high. This run is moderate risk (0.48).
risk._comment_risk_factors: Human-readable list of what contributed to the score. 'compound:' prefix = from compound signals.
risk._comment_blamed_layers: Layers identified as problematic. Each has: layer index, human-readable reasons, severity (0-1). Shown 2 of 10 blamed layers for brevity.
risk._comment_blamed_layers_flat: Backward-compatible flat list of blamed layer indices.

attention_collapse_detail._comment_mean_collapse_rate: Average fraction of collapsed heads across all layers and steps. [0, 1]. Low = healthy.
attention_collapse_detail._comment_trend_detected: True if collapse rate is increasing over generation steps (degenerating behavior).
attention_collapse_detail._comment_catastrophic: True if collapse is severe enough to warrant stopping generation.
attention_collapse_detail._comment_per_layer_mean_collapse_rate: Collapse rate averaged across all generation steps, per layer. Useful for identifying which layers are most prone to collapse.

fingerprint._comment: 25-element numeric vector summarizing this run's behavior for clustering and anomaly detection (v2).
fingerprint._comment_vector: Slots 0-4: entropy (mean,std,min,max,slope). 5-7: margin (mean,std,slope). 8-10: surprisal. 11-12: topk_mass. 13: risk_score. 14: high_entropy_fraction. 15-19: boolean flags (nan,inf,repetition,mid_layer,collapse). 20: entropy-margin correlation. 21: entropy CV. 22-23: first/last quarter entropy. 24: reserved.
fingerprint._comment_prompt_hash: SHA-256 of the prompt text. Used for deduplication and baseline lookup.

early_warning._comment: Predictive signals that detect degradation patterns BEFORE they trip health-flag thresholds.
early_warning._comment_failure_risk: Estimated probability (0-1) that the next N tokens will exhibit failure. 0.0 = no warning signs.
early_warning._comment_warning_signals: Possible signals: entropy_accelerating, margin_collapsed, margin_declining, surprisal_volatile, entropy_margin_divergence.

narrative.summary: Moderate risk (score: 0.48). Model is extremely confident with near-unanimous token selection — may be locked into a repetitive pattern. Layer 27: L2 norm outlier (z=4.0). Layer 2: Attention collapse in 67% of steps; Unstable L2 norms (CV=0.72). Layer 5: Attention collapse in 85% of steps.
narrative._comment: Human-readable 2-6 sentence summary referencing actual metric values, step indices, and tokens. Generated by narrative.py.

performance._comment: Timing breakdown of the CoreVital instrumentation pipeline. Used to measure overhead vs baseline inference.
performance._comment_parent_operations: Top-level timing phases. model_inference includes generation + timeline processing. report_build includes health flags, risk, narrative, etc.
performance._comment_baseline_ms: Time for un-instrumented generation (no hooks). Used to compute overhead.
performance._comment_overhead: CoreVital instrumentation added 23.4% overhead to inference time for this run.

seq2seq_example._comment: Seq2Seq trace from google/flan-t5-large (T5ForConditionalGeneration). Encoder-decoder architecture: has encoder_layers AND cross_attention in decoder layers. Timeline trimmed to 1 of 21 steps; layer arrays trimmed for brevity.

model._comment: T5 encoder-decoder model. 24 encoder + 24 decoder layers. Smaller hidden_size (1024) than the CausalLM example (3072).
model._comment_architecture: Seq2Seq = encoder processes input, decoder generates output attending to encoder states via cross-attention.

run_config._comment: Same config structure as CausalLM. Omitted for brevity — see causal_lm_example above.

prompt.text: Summarize: The Mediterranean diet has been linked to lower cardiovascular risk and better cognitive function in studies, but confounding factors make causal claims hard. Recent randomized trials have had mixed results.
prompt._comment: For Seq2Seq, prompt tokens are processed by the encoder. The decoder starts with a start-of-sequence token.

generated.output_text: A Mediterranean diet is healthier than a low-fat diet, but it can still be harmful.
generated._comment: T5 generated 21 tokens (including EOS token_id=1 at the end). Shorter than the 128-token max.

timeline._comment: First generation step. For Seq2Seq, step_index starts at prompt_tokens (41) because the encoder has already processed positions 0-40. Showing 3 of 24 decoder layers.

logits_summary._comment: High entropy (7.61 bits) on the first generated token — the model is very uncertain about how to begin the summary.
logits_summary._comment_entropy: 7.6 bits is very high (> 4.0 threshold). The model is choosing among ~196 equiprobable tokens (see perplexity).
logits_summary._comment_topk_probs: Top token ('The') has only 10.7% probability — no clear winner. The actually sampled token ('A') was rank 3 at 5.2%.
logits_summary._comment_top_k_margin: Only 2.2% gap between top-1 and top-2 tokens — extremely close decision. Compare to 67.8% in the CausalLM example.
logits_summary._comment_topk_mass: Top-5 tokens account for only 39.4% of probability — most mass is spread across the vocabulary. Very uncertain.
logits_summary._comment_perplexity: 2^7.61 ≈ 196. The model is effectively choosing among ~196 equiprobable tokens. Very high uncertainty.
logits_summary._comment_surprisal: The sampled token 'A' had only 5.2% probability, costing 4.26 bits of surprisal. High surprisal because a lower-ranked token was selected.

layers._comment: Decoder layer 0 of 24. KEY DIFFERENCE from CausalLM: this layer has BOTH attention_summary (decoder self-attention) AND cross_attention (decoder-to-encoder attention).

hidden_summary._comment: Note the much larger L2 norms compared to the CausalLM layer 0 (351 vs 2.6). This is architecture-dependent.

attention_summary._comment: DECODER SELF-ATTENTION. In T5's decoder at step 0, the decoder has only seen the start token, so self-attention is trivially peaked (entropy=0, all heads collapsed).
attention_summary._comment_collapsed: All 16 heads collapsed because with only 1 token to attend to, attention is deterministic. This is expected and not a health concern for autoregressive decoding.

cross_attention._comment: DECODER-TO-ENCODER CROSS-ATTENTION. This is how the decoder reads the encoder's representation of the input. Only present in Seq2Seq decoder layers; always null for CausalLM.
cross_attention._comment_entropy_mean_normalized: 0.42 = moderately spread attention over the encoder's 41 tokens. Neither fully uniform nor peaked.
cross_attention._comment_concentration_max: 0.35 = no single encoder token dominates any head's attention. Healthy cross-attention.
cross_attention._comment_cross_attn_summary: No collapsed or overly focused heads in cross-attention = the decoder is reading from the encoder in a balanced way.

layers._comment: Decoder layer 12 (middle). Layers 1-11 omitted — same structure.

hidden_summary._comment: L2 norms are extremely large (35,728) in mid-layers. This is characteristic of T5 with 4-bit quantization and contributed to mid_layer_anomaly_detected=true.

attention_summary._comment: Still all collapsed (step 0, single-token decoder self-attention).

hidden_summary._comment: Final decoder layer L2 norm drops dramatically to 13.6 (from 35,728 at layer 12). T5 applies a final LayerNorm that normalizes hidden states before the LM head.

cross_attention._comment_entropy_mean_normalized: 0.82 = nearly uniform attention across all encoder tokens in the final layer. The last layer cross-attention spreads broadly.

timeline._comment_layers: 24 decoder layers total; only layers 0, 12, 23 shown. Each Seq2Seq decoder layer has cross_attention in addition to the standard attention_summary (self-attention).

seq2seq_example._comment_timeline: Full trace has 21 steps (tokens generated before EOS). Only the first step (step_index=41) is shown.

summary._comment_total_steps: prompt_tokens + generated_tokens = 41 + 21 = 62. For Seq2Seq, prompt tokens are processed by the encoder in one pass, not step-by-step.

encoder_layers._comment: Encoder layer 0 of 24. ONLY present in Seq2Seq models. Encoder layers process the input prompt and have self-attention ONLY (no cross_attention). Run once before generation begins.

attention_summary._comment: ENCODER self-attention over the 41 prompt tokens. Unlike the decoder step-0 (which had only 1 token), the encoder sees all prompt tokens at once, so attention is meaningful.

hidden_summary._comment: Extremely high max_abs (395K). This scale is typical of T5's internal representations before the final LayerNorm.

attention_summary._comment_concentration_max: 0.9995 = one head in this layer is nearly fully focused on a single token. Could be a positional or syntactic specialist head.

hidden_summary._comment: Final encoder layer after LayerNorm — values normalized to small range. These hidden states are what the decoder's cross-attention attends to.

seq2seq_example._comment_encoder_layers: 24 encoder layers total; only 0, 12, 23 shown. Encoder layers capture how the model internally represents the input prompt. They have self-attention but NO cross_attention (that is decoder-only).

prompt_analysis._comment: For Seq2Seq models, prompt_analysis captures encoder attention patterns. layer_transformations = cosine distances between consecutive encoder layer outputs. prompt_surprisals may be empty for T5 (encoder does not produce per-token surprisals in the same way as CausalLM).
prompt_analysis._comment_layers: Each layer has 'heads' (sparse attention SoA) and 'basin_scores' (per-head, middle-third vs boundary attention ratio). Example from layer 0: basin_scores = [0.4313, 0.3583, ...] — most heads have balanced attention.
prompt_analysis._comment_layer_transformations: 23 values (24 layers - 1 transition). The last transition (0.42) is very large — the final LayerNorm dramatically transforms the hidden state. Early transitions are small (layers refine gradually).
prompt_analysis._comment_prompt_surprisals: Empty for T5/Seq2Seq because the encoder processes all tokens simultaneously (not autoregressively), so per-token surprisal is not meaningful.

health_flags._comment: This trace has multiple health concerns flagged.
health_flags._comment_attention_collapse_detected: True because decoder self-attention heads are collapsed (expected for early steps with few decoder tokens). The severity field quantifies this.
health_flags._comment_attention_collapse_severity: Variable severity [0, 1] from detect_attention_collapse. 0.4 = moderate. Only present when attention_collapse_detected=true.
health_flags._comment_high_entropy_steps: 11 of 21 steps had entropy > 4.0 bits. Over half the steps — the model was frequently uncertain during this summarization task.
health_flags._comment_mid_layer_anomaly_detected: True due to the extremely large L2 norms in mid-layers (35,000+). This is a known pattern with quantized T5 models.

compound_signals.name: attention_bottleneck
compound_signals.description: Widespread attention collapse combined with high output entropy suggests information flow is restricted.
compound_signals._comment: This signal fires when many heads are collapsed AND entropy is elevated — the model may not be routing information effectively.

risk._comment_risk_score: Maximum risk (1.0). Driven by mid_layer_anomaly (floor 0.7) + compound signal attention_bottleneck (severity 0.65) + elevated_entropy + elevated_surprisal. The continuous metrics pushed it from 0.7 to the 1.0 cap.
risk._comment_blamed_layers: 3 of 18 blamed layers shown. Layer 19 has 100% collapse rate across all generation steps.

attention_collapse_detail._comment: 10.75% mean collapse rate is elevated. The collapse trend was detected across layers.

fingerprint._comment_vector: Slot 13 = risk_score = 1.0. Slot 17 = mid_layer_anomaly = 1.0 (true). Slot 19 = attention_collapse = 1.0 (true). Slot 22 (first-quarter entropy) = 5.26 vs slot 23 (last-quarter) = 3.39 — entropy decreased over time.

early_warning._comment_failure_risk: 0.6 = moderate-high predicted failure probability. Driven by health flag passthrough (mid_layer_anomaly and attention_collapse are passed as hard ceilings).
early_warning._comment_warning_signals: These are health flag passthrough signals, not trend-based early warnings. They indicate existing problems rather than developing ones.

narrative.summary: High risk (score: 1.00), driven by anomalous behavior in middle transformer layers. Peak entropy of 7.6 bits at step 41 (mean: 3.6); the model was most uncertain when generating "A". Widespread attention collapse combined with high output entropy suggests information flow is restricted. Layer 0: Attention collapse in 86% of steps. Layer 1: Attention collapse in 52% of steps. Layer 7: Attention collapse in 62% of steps; Unstable L2 norms (CV=1.14). Consider: check input encoding, try different precision (fp32 vs fp16), or use a different model.

performance._comment: Seq2Seq performance includes encoder_forward as a separate phase. Note the much faster total time (12s vs 42s) due to fewer generated tokens (21 vs 128).
performance._comment_overhead: 62.9% overhead is higher than CausalLM (23.4%) because Seq2Seq manual decoding loop has per-step hook overhead that is proportionally larger with fewer total tokens.
